hypothesis: If a cross-modal predictive model trained to have two modalities predict the third in latent space (using L2 loss, VIC regularization, and contrastive loss) is evaluated on Matbench crystal-property tasks and AFLOW Band Gap prediction, then it will achieve lower MAE than single-modality CGCNN, CoGN, or ALIGNN baselines because integrating graph structure, textual descriptions, and XRD spectra provides more informative representations than any single modality.
